chapter_title: Motor Transp. of Migrant Agricultural Workers
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 7
### Chapter 647. Motor Transp. of Migrant Agricultural Workers
##### § 647.011 — Fuel Restrictions

(a) An operator or carrier employee fueling a motor vehicle used to transport migrant agricultural workers may not:

    (1) fuel the motor vehicle while the engine is running unless running the engine is required to fuel the vehicle;

    (2) smoke or expose any open flame in the vicinity of the motor vehicle;

    (3) fuel the motor vehicle when the nozzle of the fuel hose is not in continuous contact with the intake pipe of the fuel tank; or

    (4) permit any other person to engage in an activity that would likely result in a fire or explosion.

(b) A person may carry fuel on the motor vehicle for use in the motor vehicle or an accessory only in a properly mounted fuel tank.

*Added by Acts 1999, 76th Leg., ch. 62, Sec. 17.34(a), eff. Sept. 1, 1999.*

subsections.label: a
subsections.text: An operator or carrier employee fueling a motor vehicle used to transport migrant agricultural workers may not:
fuel the motor vehicle while the engine is running unless running the engine is required to fuel the vehicle;
smoke or expose any open flame in the vicinity of the motor vehicle;
fuel the motor vehicle when the nozzle of the fuel hose is not in continuous contact with the intake pipe of the fuel tank; or
permit any other person to engage in an activity that would likely result in a fire or explosion.

subsections.label: b
subsections.text: A person may carry fuel on the motor vehicle for use in the motor vehicle or an accessory only in a properly mounted fuel tank.

history.raw: Added by Acts 1999, 76th Leg., ch. 62, Sec. 17.34(a), eff. Sept. 1, 1999.
